reviews.review_text: Stayed two nights in a two bedroom cabin.  The rooms were clean with fresh linen and sufficient bedding provided. Kitchen had all the basics for quick meal preparation eg 2 burner cooktop, microwave, toaster, jug, saucepans, frying pan. Reverse cycle AC and wood available for the fireplace made for a cosy May stay. Good reading lights in the bedroom and plenty of hot water for showers. Staff were friendly and helpful.
Excellent place for a weekend getaway or holiday.
Very good place to stay

Great position to use as a home base, not far to town center.
Would suggest you ask what sort of site you're getting, quite a few with no slabs, alot slope abit much, and the wrong way which makes your first step into ya caravan a stretch. Amenities could of been better cleaned 🙄
Two night stay in the cabin, great spot and great service. You can buy a box of wood for $10 at the front desk that is perfect amount for one night. Will stay again.

Great park and close to everything needed, i.e. shops, servo, etc. Facilities are great, 3 camp kitchens with the main one enclosed so no bothersome flies. Ladies was kept immaculate and the showers had great pressure and hot water. 2nd shower cubicle in I found to be the best 😊 If I'm ever back that way would definitely stay again.
Very clean facilities. Great spot. Some Caravan  spots are quite unlevel and the bitumen roads within the park are in appalling disrepair.

Tourist Park well placed with sanitary facilities and clean common areas. The sites are quite good and quite shady, but it's a shame that the roads are in very bad condition to access them.

However, we were disappointed to learn that the prices (for the same duration and the same locations) were not the same for everyone. Sites with electricity indicated as $42 on your site with supposedly no locations without electricity. But some pay $27 per night, others $32 per night and unfortunately we didn't have that chance because we paid $42 for a site without water or electricity.

Too bad to give a price to the customer's head

We stayed three nights at the Top of the Town Tourist Park on our way to Crows Nest. It was the best decision we made. With powered and unpowered sites available we choose an unpowered site, as we have enough solar and battery power. The campground is much more spacious as it appears from the outside. As the campground backs onto a hill it's great to have campsites at the bottom and up on different levels. There are comon fireplaces for all to use and firewood supplied. Also a great camp kitchen with indoor and outdoor seating. The amenities  male and female are very clean and well looked after, a great deal better than other places we have seen. If you travelling through Stanthorpe or surrounding area, I highly recommend this campground.

Great little caravan park.
Very clean amenities complete with some fresh looking green plants.
Good pool for a cool down in the afternoon and close to everything in town

Lovely van park north of town. Clean and well maintained with good grassy sites and a great area at back for campers. 5 minutes to town for pubs, cafes, pharmacy eetc.Easy drive to Girraween National Park.
Great spot for a one night caravan stay and look around town

A good place to have a break. Rooms and amenities are clean and do the job nicely. The front driveway entrance and certain parts in the park need fixing. Becoming a little ptoceu now, so for it to be a better place to stay , these areas need addressing

This is a must if travelling in a van. It is like a mini Girraween on the edge of town. Nice staff, very clean, handy location to everything, quiet as a church mouse at night, very good facilities, plenty of space and a great lookout. Handy to town, Prices are very reasonable also. Dog friendly.
Really enjoy staying here.

Can't comment on rooms as we stayed in our van. Enjoyed our stay very much, staff were lovely, amenities were mostly clean as the park was very busy. Some work needed on driveway and roads but there was a water leak, probably not able to be fixed due to Xmas period and most likely going to be a big fix! Overall, I would definitely stay again so relaxed and lots of friendly guests as well 😊
Stayed in a powered site for five nights. Amenities were always clean, beautiful bush up the back of the park, and it’s in a great location close to town. Really enjoyed our stay.

We had a great time here. Excellent service over the phone and in person. We were very pleased they organised our large group with 5 different accommodation needs so well, with a fantastic ensuited site for us personally. Camp kitchen was excellent too. Highly recommend, thank you!

Good value country camp ground.
Showing its age but for the price, worth it.
Young Hayley at reception was wonderful, she got us set up in a great spot.
The park is on the side of a hill so has a few different terraced areas for tent, campers, caravans.
Easy to drive our 21 ft caravan around.
Some neat bushy areas for tents.
Drive through sites.
Plenty of cabins.
Bbq areas.
Camp kitchens.
Laundry.
Dump point.
Right next door to the showgrounds, which are not always open for caravans.
There is a little pool which looked very clean, though it was cold the day we stayed.
A short cycle into town.

We stayed at this park 21/11 to 23/11 2025 in a caravan. This caravan park has all we need and expect from a caravan park. Reception staff were friendly and helpful. There are lots of sites to choose from, some are rather small, 55 - 60 m2 in our case. Concrete slabs in many cases. There are more than one amenity  block. The one close to us was modern, all tiles and stainless steel set up and very clean. Plenty of showers, toilets and hand basins. We were impressed. There is a camp kitchen, pool/TV room and a nice little swimming pool. One can walk to town in 20 minutes, it is uphill coming home so give it 25!
Beautiful park,  clean amenities, owners/management are lovely and very helpful the kids loved the pool overall just a really good experience absolutely loved it and cant wait to return
Great park with lots of different accommodation options,  nice scenery.
Good standard park, outstanding reception 😃 good cabin

Great location & friendly service. The amenities block was clean & tidy. Someone did take my liquid bath soap within 30 minutes of me forgetting it. But it was a lovely park. A nice  walk into town. We’ll be back.
Great park,close to town. Friendly staff,great facilities
Stayed on unpowered caravan site. Lovely amenities kept spotlessly clean. Great camp kitchen. Will come again.
Clean and well maintained park sites and facilities. Nice wide roadways for site access. Friendly staff.
Busy spot, comfortable, close to town.

We booked an ensuite site for our overnight stay. Great location, friendly reception staff. The ensuite had everything you need including a perfect pressured shower. Unfortunately, it also had spiders and a ton of cobwebs over the windows but you're in a bush camp so no big deal. Lovely park, lots to explore, our kids had a great time. Very peaceful place. We enjoyed our stay and would return if we are ever back that way.

Awesome location, close to town.
Better location for wifi if you need it then the places furthet out of town.
Amazing place lovely people, great facilities, the powered sites up the top were the best of all worlds with feeling private and nice, as well as having power. 10/10!!

Enjoyed 5 days at this good location. Pleasant surroundings and good camp kitchens. No room service (change of linen, towels, removal of garbage) in the 5 days we were there. Not sure if this is a criticism as we were unaware of park policy. Showers definitely needed screens of some kind.
Terrible customer service with no credit offered when extenuating circumstances stopped us from our holiday

The service has been great.  The heater was on for us when we arrived and the cabin itself is small (normal for a standard cabin) but comfortable.  There is carpet in the living area.  We have stayed in a lot of standard caravan park cabins and
carpet is a first and very welcome in this cold weather.
We had a lovely stay and really enjoyed our Bath Haus experience. Great location to visit the wineries
Enjoyed relaxing stay at powered site.   Good clean facilities and spacious sites.   Caretaker was very helpful and friendly.
The park is very clean and well maintained definitely coming back

Stayed at the drive through sites with friends over Anzac Day weekend.  The managers were super friendly and couldn't do enough to make our stay great.  They had camp fires over the weekend which were great.  Facilities are adequate, we have what we need in our van so didn't really use them.  Camp kitchen was pretty good from what I saw.  Will definitely stay again
About 5kms out of town. We arrived on a Monday, checkin was fully automated but the cleaners were onsite and popped over to our cabin to make sure we had settled in and had everything we needed.

This was the nicest caravan park, the wine barrel baths were so relaxing. 10/10
The staff were lovely to deal with and such easy check in and out.
I stayed in the unpowered site section of the park. Wonderful campground and facilities. Close to town and also Girraween NP.

We camped for 2 nights in the off-grid camp sites. The camp kitchen was so good (it has an air fryer!), everything was clean, the bathrooms were private, like being at home. The on site bar/restaurant is so welcoming with the fireplace and everyone was so lovely. And the best bit - really good barista coffee from the hole in the wall in the morning. There's nothing I could fault, I'll definitely be back.
Perfect spot for day trips around Stanthorpe. Management team could not have been more approachable or helpful. We will stay here again.
Great park. It's location is very convenient when you are doing the wine tours.

We are a great stay in the cedar chalets. The chalets are very cute and have a lovely outlook. The service was amazing, we had a fire pit and wood delivered to the cabin along with a yummy burger and chips. The Bath  Haus is awesome too, very relaxing. We will be back.
Love our stay

Was a clean and tidy place, however communication with AI was not great. No staff on site the whole time we were there. We ordered a wine barrel bath and the temp of water was disappointing. Was not hot enough.
Had a very nice stay for may long weekend & the park staff had a all you can eat pancake breakfast for $15 which was very well done & the park & amenities were very clean & staff were very pleasant
Great facilities, clean and tidy, friendly staff, great location & reasonable prices. Food at the restaurant was good too.

Caravan park is easy and uncomplicated. Nothing fancy and makes you feel comfortable. Having fires on the sites makes it perfect for the cooler nights. Good crowd of campers while we were there. Facilities are clean and well maintained. Large sites fit van and vehicle next to it. Loved it and will be back soon.

We stayed in the 2 bedroom cottage it was a large sized space with a full size kitchen dining lounge. Bathroom separate shower. Fully screened and 2 aircon for heating and cooling.
Wood fire (would have been great had wood and fire starters were provided in the cottage) and a Broom or vacume to clean the floor. King size and queen sized beds- great warm shower. Stacks of windows and natural light.
Little outdoor area to sit and close to the BBQ and fire area. Great cooking utensils and good sized fridge. This park has a pool and has so many wonderful spots for caravans with or without ensuite and also other small medium cabins- motel like rooms. This is right beside the show grounds and is a really nice beautiful tourist park. WiFi isn't good and the tv in the room doesn't hook up to WiFi or hot spot easily.
A great spot to stay.

We are on a powered site, very level.
The amenities are 2nd to none, and the lady in reception extremely nice and helpful, I highly recommend this park, very nice and close to town.
Great Park lots of room for the dogs to go for a walk

Just the place we needed to be. We caught up with so many family and friends Attended 3 main celebrations for Anzac Day. Attended a special Birthday celebration, and then the Markets next door. Top of the Town Park was a great place to stay and so good to visit Stanthorpe again.
Nice park , facilities a very clean

Great little park at the 'top of the town' quite literally. Easy 15 min walk to the main hub, very clean amenities and good size van spaces. Service was lovely from the staff. Great option for a stop over 😀

Really disappointed in this park. We booked and paid for 3 nights in low season. ( park virtually empty) Unfortunately after the 1st night we had to return to the Gold Coast due to a medical emergency. My wife asked if the 2 nights could be refunded or credited for future use? We don't do Refunds or Credits! No worries, we don't come back and we spread the word. Far better parks without the attitude in the area.
Quite peaceful and good views from up the back of the park.

A very large park with lots of facilities that are all clean and well maintained.
Just a little far out of the town centre.
Great location friendly atmosphere and very clean amenities
Shower block could do with more attention on weekends.
Nice clean amenities, good parking space.
Lovely dog friendly camp ground.  Clean and tidy and lots of places to camp.  We will be back
Equally best caravan park we've been to. We've decided to stay for 2 weeks. The town is a 5 minute easy walk,amenities are tidy,clean and well maintained with good staff communication and clarity.
Does the job at a reasonable price with modern and clean amenities.  Would recommend.

Nice park, clean amenities and has a pool
Very friendly staff

Lovely stay close to town, the amenities were delightful and a great camp kitchen.
Laundry available and working.
Accommodating to last min change to the schedule.
A great independently run park with a wonderful atmosphere! The ensuite’s were a highlight for us with little kids as it made shower time so simple! We had a great stay, a beautiful park!

Absolute treat! We stayed in a little cabin on wheels which was parked up in the corner of the park over looking the bush and farmland. Our son, partner and dog who were in their rooftop tent, were strategically place beside our cabin.. this made our stay perfect.. we had the best of the outdoors and a gorgeous, modern, comfortable, cosy, cabin to relax in.. . added bonus was the fire pit with provided firewood which we cooked out dinner on .. . thank you so much!! We will highly recommend and will be back ourselves 🥰

Great service. A beautiful bush setting by the creek. Large communal fire pit for meeting new and old friends. Wine barrel hot tubs. And a very convenient location for accessing vineyards, The Local Wine Project, apple orchards, and adventuring through Giraween National Park. Loved it all.
Cabins very cute and comfy.  Enjoyed a nice drink and food by the fire.

Such a great family getaway, we stayed in the chalets. Very comfortable and had everything we needed.
Loved the tasting lounge!
Loved my room!

We loved out 2 night stay. We booked lounge chairs for 2 in front of the fire, so good to enjoy local wines and the tapas menu from local ingredients were delicious. We loved the bathhaus experience with cheese platter after dinner. We enjoyed a bottle of local wine. The only improvement would be some candles. Breakfast was a treat, pancakes on a sushi train style. All you can eat for $15. We will be back. We stayed in the off grid section in our van. Recommend booking a firepit, which we only saw, will book for next time.

We had a powered caravan site here. Great spot. Very welcoming staff and owners. We ate at the restaurant which showcased local food and was delicious. Also tried some local alcohol. We did the day spa whilst here which was a unique and reasonably priced experience as well. We will be back to explore more of  stanthorpe and surrounds!

Highly recommend!
We stayed at the Stanthorpe Holiday Park in a powered site over Easter and had a fantastic time.
Onsite facilities were clean and well maintained.
The park itself was lovely and well set out.
We were also lucky enough to be there for the first "tasting train" which was a great experience.
The owners are lovely people and make you feel very welcome.
Would stay here again!!
We camped off grid, great facilities and extras, friendly helpful staff all set in a beautiful area with lots to do

Miscommunication added $$ to our stay.
Staff were friendly and would go above and beyond to find you the items you needed.
I was very clear in confirming information when I spoke to a gentlemen on the phone about a week prior to our stay. In the same call I paid for a trundle and the remainder of our accomodation costs. I was told there was an electric frypan and that the fire pit was included as the accomodation came with one. We discussed the cost of firewood if I should need it and I repeated questions to confirm the fire pit didn’t need to be ordered as it was included. I had planned to order the fire pit so when I paid simply the additional $40 for the trundle, I assumed (and had verbally confirmed) I was paid up with nothing left to owe.

I ordered firewood the day before arrival via text to the AI receptionist. I asked about kindling and matches and was told the cost would be $36 for firewood, kindling and matches. This was answered by Amanda.

When we arrived there was no trundle. I asked at reception and it hadn’t been delivered or set up yet. This was approx 4pm.
It was then delivered promptly. However we had to set it up and paid $40 to do so. We needed to move furniture around to set it up in the space (again, on the phone it was mentioned to me it would fit in the space between the bed and the wall, we co sleep so it is required to go beside the bed and as it stood we actually didn’t need it as it was a Queen bed not a double). Also learnt there is no kindling kit so unsure why it was mentioned an extra $16 on top of the $20 for firewood.

There was no electric frypan. An air fryer is in this cabin. Grateful and appreciative to have one found for us given I planned our meals on this factor, but again just incorrect information given over the phone.

I was charged an extra $50 listed as fire pit and wood. Which I feel should have only been $20 given this was the information I had received via the phone call that the fire pit had no cost as was included.

Location has 3 stars as it is right next to the highway and there is alot of highway noise from large road trucks doing 100km about 20 metres from the cabin. It affected the ambience and given the little issues we had in miscommunication and extra costs I didn’t expect when I initially booked. I wasn’t having a good time and preferred to be in the quiet of my home.

We stayed over Easter 2026 in a family cabin and will definitely be going back.  The owners were very helpful and friendly.  The cabin was very neat, tidy and had been tastefully decorated. Our little dog came with us and was very welcomed. There was onsite dinner and breakfast options from the Cafe. The owners organised an Easter egg hunt for the kids and also had movie nights.
The outside surroundings and views was lovely. If your looking to stay in touch via phone calls, be aware reception was awful.

We had such a great stay at Stanthorpe Holiday Park - easily the best roadside park we’ve come across along the country corridor into Queensland.

The park is incredibly well-kept, with clean facilities, spacious sites, and a really relaxed, welcoming atmosphere. The self-service check-in was a nice touch. It strikes that perfect balance of being convenient for travellers while still feeling like a proper destination in itself.

After a long day on the road, it was such a treat to pull in somewhere that clearly takes pride in the little details. Everything just works — which, if you’ve done a few roadside stops, you know isn’t always the case.

Highly recommend for anyone travelling through - we’ll definitely be back!
Stayed over the apple and grape festival great hosts clean amenities tidy park

We just spent the weekend in the ember cabin, absolutely amazing room and style, we were so surprised walking through the door. Check in was so easy and when we caught up with staff, they were friendly and easy going, we used the bath haus and that was amazing the wife was in heaven. Park is situated just out of town so quiet and not fat either way to the tourist y stuff . Highly recommend

It was so hot we stayed a night to have pool have stayed many times
Their roadways need attention
The rooms are small, old, out-dated, smelly and extremely dirty. The main bed was full of ants. The bathroom was borderline unusable as it was so filthy.
Basic but great base to visit the attractions of Stanthorpe. Service from the reception was great.

A very large park with big sites, some drive through. Very quiet. Lovely clean amenities but the shower has pulsing water and can go very hot or cold sometimes. Not close enough to town to walk in, but there is a restaurant across the road and lovely take away like Thai and pizza in town. Lots of vineyards and national parks for day trips nearby.

Driving in was a shock with road being in such bad condition. The lock on the front door was very old and needed to be jiggled just right to get in. Tiny cabin with a fireplace still full of ashes. No lounge, just hard plastic dining chairs. Sheets were yellowing and had wrinkles from being already slept on. Shower had no soap, shampoo or conditioner. Shower curtain dripped onto bathroom floor that had no drain hole but the water flowed outside around the toilet pipe that wasn’t sealed to the floor. Hot water ran out after a very short shower (hot water tank was in wardrobe and was tiny). Winder on blinds in single beds room was missing and a bit of slat missing so couldn’t close for privacy. Dirty old hot plates and frypan. Kitchen blind had a broken slat and the window was filthy with a big old spider web in the corner that boasted a big dead fly. Boards on outside of building were floating in the wind above a broken pier. A massive bird poo (probably from a low flying duck) adorned the outside wall along with spider webs. I had to wipe down the table before we sat at it, that was shocking. Had to wipe surfaces and bed heads, totally putrid. I thought we might be able to lie on a lounge in the games room and watch telly but the lounges were covered in human remnants. We had booked for 2 night so I asked to get a refund for the second night as we wouldn’t be staying 2 nights. She told me that she didn’t think she could give a refund so I told her that she could. She finally agreed but kept $20 for some booking fee or something. I thought it was worth it to pay $20 to just not have to stay another night in that hellhole.
Rated this place 0 out of 10. We couldn’t find anything good about it. It’s funny because it’s a G’day Park and we rated  the one at Lake Boga 10 out of 10.
Really wrecked our few days away which we cut short. Shame on management.

Very clean amenities. Friendly staff .
Camp kitchen etc.
Easy to find next to Museum.
Pretty good little park and reasonable prices. Nothing much to amuse the kids but that was solved by a quick trip into town to the playground.
Great service. The food was delicious.
Great park would stay again.

The lady I met in reception was so very kind to me, I really appreciated it!! I come to Stanthorpe sometimes to see my mum who lives off grid, and I came here to buy a shower... Their bathrooms are SO CLEAN and the entire place has a very modern and well kept feel to it.

We stayed at an ensuite powered caravan site and this was our last stay over a two week adventure. I'd have to rate it as the best of the parks that we stayed at on our trip. The ensuite sites are large and located in a semi bushland setting, so we had seclusion and privacy. It was also quiet with very little traffic noise. Next time we'll stay longer than one night to explore the area.

Sites are large and well spaced around the granite rocks and facilities are great. The location is on the edge of town so very convenient to cafes, restaurants and easy access to the wineries and local attractions around Stanthorpe and Ballandean.
Great park, great facilities extended our stay as couldn’t miss a feed at the steak house opposite. Do yourself a favour & choose this park . Great friendly managers made it enjoy. 😊

What a lovely tourist park. Staff were lovely. The park was clean, very neat and tidy. Amenities were spotless. Couldn't really fault anything. Great location and pretty central. Such a variety of accommodation options from tenting to motel rooms.
Great place to stay

Online booking took all my details including credit card only then advised there was no availability.
Called to confirm and answered by a chatbot.
Reply - Amanda your 2nd scolding sms starts ' I've just found your transcript...' Neither sms, your further diatribe here or answered ph call address my concerns, your only concern is/was a review.

The manager is outstanding; unfortunately, when he is not there, the owners are catastrophic.
Their arrogance and contempt toward customers (who are the ones who make them live, but they seem to forget that) is something I have rarely seen. A simple lack of basic manners and education.
I was kicked out of this campground for “noise” (as stated in your SMS), even though I only told the truth about the owners’ practices and attitude to the wrong person.
They came to wake me up in my rooftop tent at 10 p.m. while I was sleeping and working the next day at 4 a.m., without apologizing—just banging on my car—because I was supposedly not parked in the right place. Meanwhile, someone else was playing music in the bush parking area, and no one was there to enforce the 9 p.m. curfew. ("Your homes, your rules")
This is just one story among many. Cleanliness?
When the manager is on vacation or during weekends, the toilets constantly have traces of feces, and the trash bins are overflowing.
As stated in your SMS asking for my bank details, I am still waiting for the refund of the 3 nights I was unable to enjoy, even though the reservation was fully paid on Monday.
I have been waiting for 10 days now. Are you trying to steal from me? I am seriously asking myself this question given the attitude you have shown and the excessive delay in processing the refund.
Nice big caravan park, clean amenities.  Swimming pool.  Short walk to town down the hill.
Cam & team 👌 HIGHLY recommend!
Great holiday park, good location and close proximity to the many wineries and breweries in the area.

We had a wonderful time staying here for several nights in our caravan. It was peaceful and the grounds were beautifully kept. The owners were friendly and helpful. Our little dog was welcomed and we indulged in the $10 pancake and coffee breakfast in the Cafe. Highly recommend. Great staff and a beautiful spot. We'll be back. Thank you 💕 Bruce and Tracy
very nice

Awesome stop over. I Stayed in a ensuite cabin which was very well set out. Omg the bed was sooo comfortable as I was exhausted! Enjoyed every minute of my stay. I love that the place is getting a revamp and credit to the owners for the hard work.

After a big day of exploring we decided to search for a caravan park where we could camp for the night. The caretaker was friendly and inviting. Check in was easy and quick. The back of the place is honestly a hidden treasure and could easily be the perfect place for a small event.
Only suggestion would be to have a look over the hot water system for the showers. The hot water kept coming on and off and was hard to regulate the temperature.
Owner carried bag of wood to site for me.a suggestion maybe put a screen around on site van that is attached to cabin. Overall a good stay, and yes we will come again

This place has such potential and we appreciate what the owners are trying to do.  We stayed in one of the chalets and given the rooms aren’t serviced were disappointed there was no where to hang and dry towels.  This was made worse with the poor weather.  Some extra shelving or hooks in them would help. Similarly when you know people are staying cleaning up the bird droppings from the entrance and the table and chairs would be nice and demonstrates customer care.   Self serve is fine when things are working well but that’s not always the case.  Great location and I think in time could be a great place to stay.
A work in progress. Value for money. Cafe not open until we left on Sunday morning, decent sized sites. Bathrooms were great but no servicing in 3 days
Very nice clean park well organised booking and checkin
Even though the caravan park is on the highway, this park is extremely quiet and quite relaxing, definitely stay there again.

We stayed on a powered site

The whole process of checking in was great. You can just roll in and set up no need to worry about checking in.
The female amenities are beautifully presented.
Great laundry and value for money.
To know we could go out and have our dog looked after was nice, even though we didn’t do this.
It’s the little one percenters that make this a great place to stay.
We felt comfortable and welcome thank you
It needs a dump point
3rd time going, great spot to visit wineries.|Great hosts, clean bathrooms and can always get a great coffee in the morning or nice wine and cheese at sundown.|Will be returning 😁
Loved it by the creek was very peaceful. Staff are warm and friendly.
Caravan sites were extremely hard to navigate and uneven

Park is part of the Discovery chain|Has a large number of facilities like pool,playground and fire pit|Sites are good sized but tend to be muddy and grassy so bring a mat|Amenities are clean|Showers tend to get everything wet but otherwise are large
Great place. Go there.
Good clean park great staff enjoyed my stay each time

We stayed in a family cabin which had a fire to keep us warm. The cabin was comfortable and clean and had all we needed. Was a good price for what you got. The park is beautiful with some nice walks and fire pits scattered around . The relaxing quiet nature of the park is a plus. We walked across to the Aussie Beef Steakhouse across the road which was also very good. Would recommend and would stay again.
Great place, lovely staff, very clean and modern amenities, and the ground are well maintained, thank you.

We only visited the caravan park to see the Retro cars and caravans.  We did not stay. The park is in a great spot, with van sites and cabins.  It is deceptively large.  A very neat and tidy place that even has a pool.
Close to town, great atmosphere in a quiet caravan park.
Besides the couches in the lounge room bring uncomfortable.  The cottage over all was excellent.  The staff are absolutely terrific and very well kept caravan park very nest and tidy.

I recently stayed at the G'day Park a Proud supporter of Rural Aid:
Top of the Town Tourist Park whilst we were supporting local farmers affected by the Nov 23 bushfires.

The cabin was excellent, complete with a thoughtful check-in call.
The park's location allowed for sunrise views from Mt. Marlay lookout.

Thank you for your wonderful hospitality and support during our short stay. Highly recommended!

Nice park in a convenient location, it is a little undulating, making it difficult to level the caravan on some sites and we were there on a long weekend when it was absolutely packed and some of the sites are quite squishy. The amenities block is in great condition and kept well cleaned and maintained. And as usual for us it is pet friendly. So much to do around here, everything from the truffle farm, juicery, breweries, a whole lot of wineries and many other things.
Well kept caravan park.  Plenty of room to get a van onto a site.  Lovely clean amenities.
Nice caravan Park reasonably close to the main shopping area. You can walk there if you want to.
A very nice caravan park. Very clean amenities. Dog friendly.

Great van park. Nice roomie sites so you can put your car on the other side of the slab. Always a bonus. Amenities were very clean and plenty of them. Great camp kitchens and BBQ sites dotted around the park AND it was close to everything 🩷
Looks nothing like the photos.not really catered towards tents more for camper vans The only redemption was the toilet blocks they were really clean . Also too many rules everywhere .
Stayed 3 nights, quite camp, albeit close to the road, friendly staff, amenities were spotless, no river due to drought, 20 minutes from Girraween National Park for spectacular walks
Have just  returned from 4 nights at this park. Immaculate facilities and park. Nice spacious sites. Hosts Ben & Narelle were fantastic. Will be back again.

Such clean facilities,  very friendly and welcoming environment.  Went with a family group camping and had a great time . Took my doggy Bronson and he loved it there , dog off lead playground was awesome to have .
Communal fire at night was fun
Highly recommended place to stay .

The hosts, Narelle and Ben, are very friendly and accomodating. The property is suffering from a long period without rain but this does not distract from the camping experience. We had a great stay, Thnak You!! PS beware of the trees, some tend to jump out in front of your caravan to commit suicide.

A great location. Ben and Narelle were great hosts explained and gave suggestions on sites around Stanthorpe to visit. Had park get together around the park fire pit most nights. Ben was fantastic with a problem I had with the van and was able to source someone to repair it. Would fully recommend a visit to park. Great place great atmosphere
This caravan park an absolute gem, the owners Ben and Narelle are the most amazing hosts. The sites are big, amenities are spotless and the camp kitchen at another level, has everything you need.

Quite and friendly caravan park. Toilets and showers are clean and well maintained. $1 for 6 minute shower- great water pressure and nice and hot. River was low because of current drought but made it easier to explore 🦆🪾🏕️

The caravan park is in a pretty spot along the Severn River, about 5mins south of Stanthorpe and close to many wineries. Facilities were kept clean and the bins emptied every day. Level drive-through sites with space. Staff friendly and helpful.

We loved our stay here, great location right opposite Winery and short trip into town with plenty to explore in this amazing granite belt. Beautifully clean facilities and hosts Ben and Narelle were fantastic. Being pet friendly, the off leash area for our dog to exercise was a plus. Would highly recommend and will certainly return. Thanks guys!
Great few days away lovely setting and amazing hosts thoroughly enjoyed the evening communal fire pit hosted by the team. Lots of banter and stories. Great clean facilities. Highly recommend 👍

The caravan spaces are roomy and the off grid area near the river provides lovely views with lots of wildlife.  The river was low when we visited due to lack of rain but it still had water and ensured all manner of birdlife right on your doorstep.||The showers were a bit hit and miss and are coin operated due to water restrictions however the facilities are very clean and there is a well equipped camp kitchen.||Kominos winery is directly across the road and have great wine and lovely cheese platters if you are looking for a memorable lunch spot to while away the afternoon.

Great location and lovely park. The whole area was very dry when we visited which does have an impact but lots to do in the area. Ben and Narelle were excellent hosts and we'll definitely be back again. Really enjoyed our stay.
Lovely hosts.  Pity the was not much water in the weir but we enjoyed the outlook and the bird life.  Would highly recommend
Lovely hosts.  Pity the was not much water in the weir but we enjoyed the outlook and the bird life.  Would highly recommend

Very clean park, quite at night.
Park owners/Manager’s were very polite and friendly always up for a chat.
Would recommend this park for an overnight stay or longer
A nice place to stay

We booked this campground months in advance & had booked a riverside caravan unpowered sites, but as the Stanthorpe area hasn't had any rain since November, the river had dried up to a couple of puddles, it was still a nice sites & the camo hosts were great, we would definitely go back

Great spot, really convenient and great food.

Overall the staff is really nice.

We stayed with a group of friends over Easter and had a lovely weekend.  Owners were great and very helpful.  Love how they work with other local businesses arranging buses to take us to a winery for dinner.  And a cheeky cocktail beforehand at the Cafe was great.

We loved our stay at Stanthorpe Holiday Park! The little baths with the platter were such a special touch, and it was wonderful to enjoy our own fire pot on site. Amanda and Blake are doing an amazing job running the park. Having recently taken over, they’ve embraced the challenges and are already making impressive strides. The park itself is set in the beautiful wine region of Stanthorpe, and while it still needs some work, their dedication and passion are evident in every improvement. We were so impressed by how welcome they made us feel. It’s inspiring to see young people giving it a go and succeeding, and we want to encourage and support them as they continue to enhance this lovely spot. We’ll definitely be back — highly recommend!

We really enjoyed our 3 nights stay back in April. It was nice to be just be out of the hustle and bustle and closer to wineries etc on the southern side of town. We had a lovely site backing on to a paddock with views of a spectacular sunset. The park was reasonably quiet and despite being on the highway you didn’t hear any of the passing traffic from our site.  It’s close to the brewery too. The owners seem to be making continual improvements so we look forward to seeing what they’ve done on our next visit!

How the lady working there treated my mother who is 70 years old is absolutely disgusting. We were passing through and she abused us. We will never stay here and we are on a much needed weekend away as healthcare workers and we did not need to be treated like animals. We know many people in Warwick in the camping industry and Gold Coast and will tell everyone not to stay here !!! 😠

What a beautiful property. We stayed for 2 nights in one of the chalets and absolutely loved the vibe. The owners/managers of park are nice and always happy to help. Clean toilets, large grassy area and creeek flowing through the property. Definitely give it a try if you are planning to visit Stanthorpe. 5 mins from shops/cafes.

Tent camping par excellence.
Best showers, best tent site.

The hosts were so accommodating. The bathrooms were really clean. Although we didn't stay in a cabin, they looked really cool. Lovely location and even though it is on the highway, the noise was not bad and we got a good nights sleep.

This is a lovely park, it trucks in the water so water pressure can be a little slow and the WIFI  kept dropping out, otherwise we thoroughly enjoyed our stay at the park. We spent 9 days at this lovely holiday park. It is well maintained, clean and tidy bathroom, laundry. Nice to walk around the park area and down to the creek. Keep up the good work and we hope you have fun with your little house you got delivered while we were there.
Really nice place to go. There’s heaps to do and you won’t be disappointed at all. Go on, head to Stanthorpe and check it out.

You can tell the new owners have put alot of effort into making this a special place for families to come and enjoy. Everything was clean and cosy. The additional pine trees recently planted, fire pit near the river and thought out menu are all nice touches that made the experience special.

We stayed on a powered site over the weekend and had a fantastic experience at Stanthorpe Holiday Park. The owners were warm and welcoming, and the park itself was spotlessly clean with well-maintained facilities throughout.

The location is excellent — peaceful, yet conveniently close to Stanthorpe’s main attractions and local wineries. A real standout was the Pancake Breakfast and Coffee on Sunday morning — a lovely touch that made the stay even more enjoyable.

Everything from the clean amenities to the spacious sites and overall relaxed vibe made this a great getaway. Highly recommend to anyone visiting the Granite Belt region.

Great little place to stop around stanthorpe. Easy to locate with a great little creek
Amanda and Blake were so welcoming. The facilities were second to none. The Bath Hause experience … amazing. Can’t wait to do this again with my beautiful girlfriend. We didn’t want to leave.

I've lost count of how many times we've stayed at Stanthorpe Holiday Park. We've set up our caravan on many sites and even stayed in a cabin. The facilities are always clean and well maintained. Nice quiet caravan park. Lovely big clean showers, amenities always clean. The back of the park has bush camping so makes for lovely walks.

Good location but we stayed in the cottage paying a pretty hefty price for what we got. There was mould in the shower that had definitely not been cleaned before us. Nice park , with a large site , staff friendly , it’s in a great location to explore the area . Quiet spot, make sure you book ahead
Great Caravan park and very friendly staff. Pet friendly as well. Will definitely stay again

A vile place, completely run down to the point of being derelict.

Customer service was very bad. We were allocated a campervan spot right next to the function room where early 20 somethings were having a christmas party, drinking and karaoke. Obviously it was very loud that kept our young kids awake until 10:15pm.

After contacting reception, we were told they can be as loud as they like until 10pm. As for us, tough luck! Fair enough you have christmas parties but do you really put a family with young children right next to it instead of moving them to a space well away.

Loved being woken up the next morning at 4:50am with the same party music thumping out until 5:15am.

Poor attitude, poor service and very dilapidated place, yuk!

Powered caravan sit was great. Receptionist was well mannered and very helpful. Park is kept neat and tidy and pool clean and refreshing. Location is prime when exploring the region and sights. Restaurant in walking distance if you don't want to cook.

What a peaceful place... walking around the Hida old town was so  relaxing after Takayama. We look for authentic experienced. If you go to Toyama hop a train to Hida then takayama. Fantastic place. Plenty of different types of accommodation. Toilets amd shower extremely clean. Make sure you book well in advance as is often booked out. Close to town and Sunday markets next door 2nd and last Sunday od each month.

Amenities were ok at best with faily leaning lacking detail. Look above the shoulders.

Just out of town on the new England. Get to experience the stars at night and
pretty creek running through. We were with the van but the on site accom looked quaint yet lovely. A few make you stay special type experiences for a few look nice too. Flet it was more for the oldies then those with young kids. It was proper cold this winter.

We just spent 2 nights in Chalet 3. Cute and cosy little chalet. Amenities were clean and tidy. $1 hot showers for the win. Cooler weather meant we got see a stunning frost over the park this morning. We also tried the bath haus, very cute idea and enjoyed it. Water was a little dirty though. Only fault was lack of communication from owners, we msgd about a couple of things and received no response. Anyways great to way to spend our 25th anniversary.

Do not stay here.
The grounds are nice but there was a lot of noise from the highway and gunshots could be heard frequently at night. The amenities are decent but required more cleaning as they were almost always dirty. There were no paper towels in the toilet block closest to the unpowered camping and no hand dryers either. The coin powered showers say you can put multiple coins in for continuous use but the water always shut off early. The camp kitchen didn’t even have a toaster and there didn’t seem to be any gas connected to the pizza oven. For the price of the fire pit hire, you could buy your own. We could have overlooked all of this if it wasn’t for the owner Amanda ruining our stay. On Saturday afternoon she stormed into our camp and started interrogating us. Apparently, someone with a white dog used the baths without paying at 1pm. She profiled us because our dogs are partially white and accused us of using the baths without paying. We had in fact paid to use the baths earlier in the day - don’t recommend doing as it’s overpriced for what it is. The water had bugs and debris floating in it and the platter is tiny (a handful of crackers, 2 strawberries and 2 small slices of cheese). We also didn’t get the bottled water. There was broken glass on the floor which we cleaned up for them. We explained to her that we booked the baths at 3pm and thought that was it. Then a couple hours later, she came by to harass us again and asked if we were sure that we weren’t the people who used the baths at 1pm. We weren’t even in the park at that time. We were trying to have a nice weekend away for our anniversary and the new owners turned it into a stressful and negative experience. Please don’t make the same mistake we did in staying here.

Great private park.
Facilities clean and very nice.
Arrived early, but accommodated us anyway.  Very helpful.
Will definitely stay again.

A big group of us (about 30) just stayed here in mid July for my 40th birthday Christmas in July celebration. We have a lovely Riverview site. It was a white Christmas in the morning with beautiful sunny days and we all had so much fun. Everyone who stayed with us commented on how beautiful the woman’s bathroom was and how cool the cafe was. The river was a favourite with the teenagers and tweens! So close to cheese places and wineries as well as Girraween national park. We had a games afternoon on the big grass area by the river before gathering around our fire. The icing on the cake for me was that the showers had nice hot water. We will definitely be back!!!

Camping in a group on both powered and non powered. Cannot fault this park, the facilities and the staff. The owners are very friendly and went over and above to accomodate us. The park is unique in its offerings, with daily happy hours, great food, saunas, bath houses and camp fires. If we didn’t have a caravan, the Chalets look amazing to stay in. Thanks again, will definitely be back.
We camped with friends and we could not fault this park.  Owners were friendly accommodating.  We would recommend this park

Staying 5 nights this week we arrived with high expectations from all the great reviews. Being school holidays I would have expected a slightly higher level of accessibility to staff.|We checked in via a text which was fine but did try to check in physically several times over our stay but met with the office being closed. This led to lack of information around accessing features of the park. QR code directed info and booking of features is hard with poor internet access through mobile and wifi. I did check on my first and second day on the bath haus still to find the same rubbish left by a previous user and not cleaned out. It made me wonder how clean the water would be. The communal fire was also never lit in our 5 night stay during the school holidays. I’m not sure if the lady cleaning was a staffer or owner but seemed very unprofessional driving around in the buggy smoking on majority of occasions we crossed paths. To me the park seemed very superficially dressed to impress but lacked the depth and care that travellers seek. We enjoyed our stay and were satisfied with our site and amenities. Value for money is ok. Park is quite nice overall with space for kids to ride bikes and kick balls around. River is nice to explore and access to Stanthorpe and local area is easy by car. Road noise isn’t great but it’s expected on the main road out of town.

What a beautiful stay! Location is perfect and right by a gorgeous creek that adds to the charm. We loved the fire pit in the evenings and the facilities were clean and well maintained. The staff were incredibly friendly and the service was outstanding. We’ll definitely be back for another stay! Highly recommend.

Very good location, awesome park.
You can pamper yourself here as well.

We stayed for 7 nights for the Stanthorpe winter festival 2025 and wow !!! Bath haus, sauna, pet friendly and the kids loved climbing over rocks at the creek. The food in the cafe is all local and next level good! Bathrooms are super clean. The owners are beautiful people who would honestly go above and beyond to ensure you have a cosy stay. We would not hesitate to book again. Highly recommend!
Great owners and excellent facilities

We were going to stay here but they were so rude and unaccommodating when arrived we left and went to the “country style holiday park” which was awesome.
They then charged me for my stay 3 days later and said it was an error as they didn’t mark the booking as cancelled. Give this place a wide birth.

Beautiful location , terrible amenities for caravan users.  Level of cleanliness in communal showers and toilets was very poor. Broken seats in 2 out of 3 women's  showers . No paper towels  or hand dryer in another shower area in the 4 days we were there. Toilets not cleaned. Rubbish  bins  in this area only emptied   on day 4  Rubbish bins in camp kitchen overflowing. Did not mind paying to have hot  shower , and the location was lovely . Maintenance and cleanliness not up to standard

The chalet exceeded our expectations.  It was so cosy, the bed was very comfortable and there was even two complimentary bottles of water in the fridge.  Sadly we didn’t get time to try the wine barrel spas but they looked as good as the picture in the ad.  The river is a short walk from the chalet and apparently is a good fishing spot.  The only downside is the separate amenities but the charm of the chalet makes up for this.
Lovely camp site for van

Great value for a 3 night stay, with good sized unimpeded powered sites. Good location to explore all the offerings of Stanthorpe. Good communication from the park and an easy hassle free check in. The waffles and coffee at the cafe quite lovely. The park has some good offerings, such as fires, a firepit and the bath haus. The ensuite style amenities are good, however we found they could have been cleaned more frequently, same with the camp kitchen.

Good sized sites, huge park, great views.
Amenities were awesome, spotless, plenty of sinks, shower cubicles and toilets.
Large amount of sites with amazing views.
Just not many bins.

Stayed in a cabin. Clean but bare with most basic of amenities provided. Asked for a long life milk for the second day and was given it most reluctantly with the comment, we don't supply the milk for the second night stay.
Nice big caravan / motor home / big rig park. Clean big amenities. Plenty for the kids to do. Super friendly helpful staff.

Spent one night here in our caravan.
Great spot. Spotless amenities. Would recommend.
Stayed for 4 nights in an unpowered campsite. It had great amenities, clean toilets, and showers. We definitely enjoyed our stay here. No dramas. Good customer service.
Unpowered site at back of park. Stunning views. Felt like camping in the bush with all of the amenities of a well run and clean caravan park. Perfect.

Have always loved staying at this lovely caravan park. However, having to listen to uncontrolled yapping dogs, spoilt it for us this time. For some reason , some people with small dogs think continuous yapping is okay???? Didn't hear any if the larger dogs barking at all.
On site cabin was in very poor condition, extremely small inside and run down. Would not recommend.
Very friendly and helpful
Spacious caravan park. Super clean amenities block & very friendly helpful staff. Lovely camp kitchens. Definitely recommend.

Great spot to stay to start your exploration of the region. Close to town and shopping. Good value. Located right on the New Engalnd Highway. Lovely couple run the park and are very friendly and accommodating. They have recently purchased the park and are working hard to get it up to standard. Nice spot near a small creek/river. Cabins, drive through powered sites and other types of accommodation. There is so much to do in the area, wineries, food and site seeing, and great national parks with awesome bush walking.
Awesome place to stay. Good dinner good service good view

Spent 5 nights here recently whilst on a quick work trip down in Stanthorpe, decided to take the caravan so my wife could join me for the trip.  I cannot fault this park, upon arrival Amanda was so welcoming and spoke to my wife in detail about recommendations for wineries and other local attractions.  Both Amanda and Blake were excellent throughout our stay, and I have booked back in for 12 nights for another upcoming trip.  The Spa and Bath Haus were amazing and such a nice touch to a caravan park.  Everything was well maintained and even during a long weekend it was quiet and peaceful.  There are so many little touches that make this place special and I cannot wait to get back down there again.  Thank you to Amanda and Blake and family for your hospitality, look forward to spending may weekends down with you all.  Richard & Lisa

We had the most wonderful stay at this beautifully maintained caravan park. The new owners—a lovely young family—are clearly passionate about creating a warm, welcoming space, and their hard work is already showing. The service was absolutely fantastic from start to finish.|The park itself is immaculate, with large, grassy sites that offer plenty of space to relax. The amenities are modern, clean, and well thought out, especially the camp kitchen, which is spotless and fully equipped.|The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful, always happy to assist with a smile. One of the highlights was the on-site café—the food was delicious and the wine selection surprisingly impressive for a park café!|We can’t wait to return and see what else this amazing family does with the place. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a peaceful, friendly, and well-run park.
Great to see what new owners have achieved with this park
Great little spot with awesome hosts. Would recommend to all and will definitely be back

We had the most wonderful stay at this beautifully maintained caravan park. The new owners—a lovely young family—are clearly passionate about creating a warm, welcoming space, and their hard work is already showing. The service was absolutely fantastic from start to finish.
The park itself is immaculate, with large, grassy sites that offer plenty of space to relax. The amenities are modern, clean, and well thought out, especially the camp kitchen, which is spotless and fully equipped.
The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful, always happy to assist with a smile. One of the highlights was the on-site café—the food was delicious and the wine selection surprisingly impressive for a park café!
We can’t wait to return and see what else this amazing family does with the place. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a peaceful, friendly, and well-run park.

Max & Sophie’s adventures around Oz recently spent one week in this great little van park.  It is located a short distance  from town, but don’t let that stop you.  We arrived on Anzac Day and the place was packed,  there was a great Texas BBQ tent there for the long weekend, and entertainment with a great duo, which all made such a fabulous vibe.
We were able to take advantage of the Bath Haus, which included a cheese platter.  Such a unique experience enjoying a soak in a wine barrel while watching the sunset.   My only constructive criticism would be regarding the Bath Haus, where despite having made a booking we just felt a little unexpected or unplanned for.   A conversation with another lady in the park who also booked the Bath Haus, they did not receive the cheese platter.  Just a little more preparedness to have the Bath Haus set up for those who have booked.  That said, we still very much enjoyed the experience.
The amenities are all upgraded and quite unique.
There is a lot of work going into the park and it shows all the promise of being a real gem, in the great ‘foodie’ town of Stanthorpe.
Most definitely recommended.

Such a great place and very relaxing. Great service and very friendly people running it. We had the eggs benny toastie for breakfast and it was amazing! Coffee was great too! In the evening we had the smoked apple whiskey which was awesome! Highly recommend and we can't wait to go back.

Absolutely loved our stay here! The location is perfect—peaceful, scenic, and still close to everything you need. The amenities really set this place apart, especially the sauna and bath haus, which added such a relaxing touch to the whole experience. The two owners were incredibly welcoming and hospitable.  You can really tell they put a lot of care into creating a calm yet still engaging atmosphere as there was always something going on to keep you entertained. Highly recommend

Nice little eco park not too far out of town.  Bit of truck traffic in the night but that didn't bother us.  New owners Amanda and Blake are lovely and welcoming.  Nice surroundings with a river down the hill.  Lots of interesting packages to choose from like couples sauna and massage chairs, platters, picnic baskets and firepit and wood hire etc.  A nice extra touch.  Water is trucked in therefore showers are $1.  The only thing missing is a dump point.  You need to go into town for that in a busy spot which is a bit tricky with the van.
Stayed in cabin and a chalet over the last couple of days. Absolutely beautiful. Welcoming staff and a good night.  Thank you so much for my stay I will definitely be back.

Great spot to stop on your travels

nice size lots for caravans
beautiful rustic park with river on the back

lovely staff lovely young family trying to make a go of it

Amazing holiday park! There's a lot of options from glamping in cabins, to bringing your own caravan, to off-grid tenting. Very well maintained. Decent prices. The restaurant is lovely too. Highly recommended, I will be returning.

Absolutely love my stay. Beautiful,  peaceful n relaxing. The river, lagoon n relaxing peaceful nights.  A little piece of paradise in today's busy world.

Great little caravan park. Young couple working so hard . Bathrooms clean with extras if you forgot something. Don’t forget your only minutes away from wineries breweries and national parks. Thanks again Mandy and Blake.
We stayed 3 nights in our Motorhome on a Riverside powered site. Very relaxing. Blake & team are wonderful

On arrival was met by friendly owners. Was given caravan site overlooking Severnlea River, pure heaven fresh air and starry sky at night.
Very clean amenities and well maintained grounds.
Meals can be purchased from the on-site Cafe. My partner enjoyed the parmigiana and l had fish & chip (barra) beautiful presentation,  also licensed to buy alcohol.
Would definitely recommend 💯 % we will be back. Thanks
Loved going down there. Staff are super friendly. Absolutely beautiful spot. Can't wait to go back again.

**EDIT** I have been contacted by the operators and informed the coffee cups are a clear plastic/glass cup with no handles. I will accept my original comment was not correct. The finishing touches and trims to the cabin weren't completed as trades have let them down. By no means is this the fault of the park. I have changed my rating to an all round 4 star. Given time this place will have alot of potential and should be a nice place to stay. I would stay here again.

I can look past the fact the cabin build hasn't been finished yet, I like the raw timber look personally. What has really set me up for a bad day is as I went to make my morning coffee I found there are no coffee cups!

Located 10 minutes south of Stanthorpe. $37 pn off peak for powered sites. Lots of space. Well equipped camp kitchen, just needing a TV for campers. Very green, with campsites by the river. Cabins are clean. The park has some facilities i had never seen at a park before. Picnic baskets available for purchase, stocked with local produce. Owners are welcoming and friendly. I would stay here again.

A lovely little park new owners doing a really good job cleaning it up

We loved our stay here!
The location was ideal for us. The new managers are so friendly and accomodating (especially making cocktails and meals, explaining the local products used from the area :)).
We are impressed with all the love put into the park, highlights being the cute chalet, sauna, barrel baths. Was a nice afternoon at the fire pit too. Looking forward to spending more time here when we are next the area. Thank you!
Absolutely unique camp experience. Enjoy soaking in the Bath Haus, sunset and fire pit at the river, sauna room and tasty food at the cafe. Highly recommended

A great little Caravan park with a very cosy feel with lovely hosts and amazing on-site Cafe. The Owners are doing a great job.

Had a wonderful weekend at this little gem. The family who runs the park are delightful and go out of their way to help. The amenities are spotless. Couldn't believe how quiet it was, being next to the highway. Will be back.

The nights away in this beautiful cabin
Stunning work done in the bathroom and interior of the cabin
Air conditioning and a heater
Coffee pod machine and little trinkets to make our stay extra special
While the trucks on the highway were a little loud Thursday and Friday nights, always less traffic on Saturday night, which made for a great night. Noise is expected when you stay on a highway.
The breakfast at the Cafe was spot on and even had oat milk for an oat milk shake, winning.
The toasted sandwiches were scrumptious.
A perfect spot to day trip all around Stanthorpe.
Thank you

amenities but saying that, very clean and  welcoming.

Staying here currently. Great spot, convenient to the local attractions around Stanthorpe. Spotless facilities. Wow- where to begin.|We booked a powered site for the long weekend and did not know which site we had until arrival. We were given a full gravel site- and even though they mentioned leveling these sites due to old grass bumpy sites, I think they should have left them. Although it states WIFI- we had one bar that never worked. They advertise Hot baths for couples. These were not working over a busy long weekend. Great Location, Friendly owners, Clean Ammenties! What more could you ask for!

We have just spent a week at this lovely park on an unpowered riverside site and booked our next stay before leaving (which should tell you something).

Sites:
I typically don't like staying in caravan parks because they tend to be small sites on top of each other but these sites are large and spaced out so you get your own space from other guests and I was so pleased.

Owners: From the moment we arrived the owners could not have been friendlier. They work hard to present the park well for their guests and try to cater for everyone (cabins, tents and vans etc). I'm going to miss our afternoon chats next week when back at work haha.

There are farm animals the kids can feed and an off leash dog area which is a fantastic idea.

Location: Perfect location with easy access to wineries and my fav cafe Jamworks.

River:Yes the River is very low due to the drought (if you look around the area you can tell it's been awhile since they've had rain - I think a local wine maker said they've had no rain since November!) but that just means you get the opportunity to fossick in the river bed for Topaz and Quartz. My screen obsessed son had hours of fun doing this.

Storm King Dam is only about 15 minutes away if you want to kayak and fish meaning you can have the best of both worlds.

Fires: Presently you can't have open fires around the park which when you look at how dry everything is and how little water is the only smart thing to do.

But wait, you can still bring along those marshmallows and toast them on the communal fire!

The owners lit the communal fire each night we were there for everyone to enjoy while still being able to keep an eye on it so as not to start a grass fire (which is super easy to happen when this dry and left unsupervised).

Overall we had a lovely week and am already looking forward to our next trip.

Quick clarification after the owner’s response: Local businesses told us the water was “drained” to support nearby capsicum farms. We understand and respect the park’s decisions, which is evident in my review (including the no-fire policy) — our only concern was not being given a heads-up before arrival so we could prepare or adjust our plans, regardless of any cancellation fees. Also, after reading several low reviews, it appears the owner often focuses on finding issues with their guests rather than taking on the feedback, which I found telling.

No water in the creek & no fires made this a mixed stay.
The good: the camp kitchen is good, the showers/toilets were clean and plentiful, and the showers are $1 for 5 minutes which is very generous (no issues with this at all). The dog park is also a big plus. There is a nice winery across the road. We also appreciated that the tent allocations felt quite spaced out and not overcrowded. It didn’t feel like they were trying to overbook just to maximise numbers.
No water in the creek & no fires (and why it matters): when we arrived, the creek/river was completely dry. It was made out to be that it had just dried up naturally, but later we heard from locals that the water had actually been diverted to support farms during low rainfall. I completely understand that’s out of the park’s control, but we really wish we’d been told prior to arrival, as the water was one of the main reasons we booked.
Same with fires: the place is clearly very dry, so a no-fire policy is completely fair. However, this appeared to be a park-specific rule rather than a state-enforced restriction, and again, a heads-up would’ve helped. We came camping expecting a creek and a fire, and ended up with neither.
Biggest issue: we were placed near the sewage treatment/tank area and unfortunately it stunk, plus the tank noise kicked on and off through the night and kept waking us up.
This park is probably better suited to caravans than tent camping (at least based on where we were put). If you do stay, ask to be camped well away from the sewage area. Also note it’s close to the highway, so expect road noise.

Location was great close to everything a family wants to see.|The manager was super friendly and very helpful, he made our gbabies first camping trip easy.|Well worth the money.|Bathrooms and grounds kept very clean and clear of littler.|Noise level was typical of a camp ground but nothing loud after 2100.
