We were the only customers in the store and the tables were dirty and sticky.

I’m honestly shocked by how much the standards have dropped in both of these venues after their expansion/relocation. It really is a shame as there is nowhere else locally where you can source the same variety of food.

This was a hidden gem. We were passing through Stanthorpe and came across this little diner. The food and atmosphere was really good. Kids loved their burgers the fries were great and loved the chilli dog. Will definitely be back here next time in Stanthorpe.
Excellent range of burgers n fries, and beers on tap!

Blue Topaz Caravanpark. It’s a place where a workation turns into a dream.
Stayed here for 6 months in 2017 and another time this year for around 8 months.
This place combines not just the typical backpackers life with a feeling of beeing home, it furthermore also gives local travelers the chance to make friends around the world.
Not only the owners Brendan, Shonel and her lovely family are a reason to stay, also their absolutely heartwarming way to deal with people, their unbelievable skills in making food and the perfect location of the park right next to severn river are guaranteeing an unforgettable stay - no matter how long it’ll be.
This is a place to enjoy peace, freedom, nature and people and above all - the worlds best burgers and the most fanciest food we’ve seen on our trips around the world.
The very unique diner is unbelievable detailed and people with heart can directly tell, that it’s pure passion and love running this business.
We’re so thankful to have been here, that it really hurt when we left.
Feels like we left a piece of our life there.

What a gem! We stayed two nights in the ‘bush camping’ area (unpowered) and loved it. The river was a few steps down and just gorgeous with heaps of bird live to watch. You can swim, however because it hasn’t rained for a while, I won’t recommend.
The amenities were a short walk away, very clean with $1 coin showers, which we didn’t mind at all as it is very water vise in my opinion.
They also have goats and sheep, which was such a fun to watch the two baby goats play with each other and being silly.
The whole campground is set in a beautiful landscaped property and very well maintained. You can have your own fire in the fire pits on the river bank side, however due to fire restrictions couldn’t, but had a great evening at the communal fire pit with the owners and other campers.
There’s also powered sites and cabins.
One other thing worth to mention is the location of the campground,  there’s plenty of wineries, breweries and distilleries around, however there’s a winery across the road if you don’t want to drive 🍷 🤩
A man made pyramid near by is a little attraction, so is the Ballandean Pub and Motel with a great beer garden and live music on weekends. Newly renovated it’s a must. (Cannot comment on food as we only had drinks)
The owners of Country Style Caravan Park are such nice people, really caring about the grounds and people and so welcoming.
Thank you so much for your hospitality 💕, we will be back!

Beautiful, clean, friendly, quiet and very welcoming caravan park. great location, beautiful river view (with options to swim and paddle board) our kids loved helping out with the goats too. Thank you Narelle & Ben for such a welcoming experience and we look forward to seeing you soon 😊

Stayed 4 nights. Grounds and facilities are some of the most well maintained and clean I have come across. The river is beautiful and calm, great for paddle boarding or kayaking. Close to Stanthorpe for cafes or supplies. Definitely recommend staying here.

Beautiful area to camp (we were in a tent), and the only downside is the occasional sound of vehicles travelling on the highway. Will be very happy to come back here in the future!

Well-kept facilities (seriously, they keep these bathrooms clean and tidy) and grounds. I see the staff out and about a lot every day doing all kinds of care for the grounds,

Friendly staff,

Cash and card available at office
office. They also exchange cash for cash if you need coins for the showers ($1 for 6 minutes fyi),

Office has ice bags and goat food available for purchase at reasonable price. No groceries available for purchase,

Goats and sheep on site in pens (not a petting zoo),

Laundry room facilities: washers and dryers. Coin operated. Can't remember price. Clothesline outside. Has a small stocked bookshelf too,

Communal areas:
Giant chess game board and pieces,
Fire pit and seating,
Camp kitchen equipped with microwave, kettle, toaster, shelving, 2 bbqs, stove,
Fenced dog area with ramp and things for them to do.

15 mins from Stanthorpe shopping area which has kmart hub, cafes,  restaurants,  pharmacy, banks, and heaps of other things.

Fishing: not much! Might be seasonal, and us newbies don't know much about that. But here there have been only a handful of fish visible (while stand up paddle boarding), and hubby has fished twice daily for 3 days and caught nothing.

Water is beautiful though! Not too cold (it is summer now), and have seen children swimming in the river enjoying themselves. Rocky area behind the river dam wall to explore.
We had a great time in country style campsite. Beautiful lake view. Great for Kayaking!! Great for family stay. Very kids friendly.  Thank you for that

Just spent a week at Country Style Holiday Park.
This Caravan Park has great hosts, Ben and Narelle, they are friendly and very helpful.

The park has a relaxed atmosphere, with some sheep and goats to pat and feed, plus a nice walk down to the river within the park. We stayed on the large drive through sites at the top of the park. Great giant chess game to enjoy also.

The  2 Amenity blocks were always clean.

The location is just south of Stanthorpe and close to Wineries and other local sites. Even a winery across the road to enjoy.

We would highly recommend this park and looking forward to our next visit.

Great sites by the river which is good for kayaking, good facilities, close to nice vineyards (incl one straight across the road), giant chessboard which was novel. Goats and sheep. Good cycling conditions for kids. Shall return!
Fox bar Falls it’s a lot better

This holiday park is top shelf. Our hosts Ben and Narelle are absolutely delightful. The whole place is neat and tidy and our cabin was spotless. The amenities were clean and the pet friendly atmosphere was second to none. My wife and could not rate this more highly. 10 out of 10. Thankyou for making our stay so special. Mark and Lee Lewis.

We had a lovely stay at Country Style Holiday Park. We were on a powered site which was a great size. The amenities were clean and the grounds lovely and well kept. We travelled with two dogs and the off-leash area was very handy. It was overall a great place to stay.

Absolutely fantastic place to set up camp, right along the river, goats and sheep are friendly and the baby goats are too cute. The site is beautifully maintained and the hosts are so friendly and helpful. The amenities are top notch, clean and well placed. I really cannot recommend highly enough
Stayed at a power site, we were a large group and they gave us sites right next door to each other. Enjoyed our stay there.

River was very disappointing. Lots of weed, fairly stagnant water. Water was pretty muddy close to shore and access was very limited due to van sites. Access to water via the weir was very slippery and not suitable for littlies. Very different to their website photos.

This was a fabulous caravan park with excellent hosts ! Thank you for a wonderful home away from home !
Sites - excellent
Amenities- excellent
Customer care - excellent
Attention to detail - excellent
Campers behaviour- excellent.
We couldn’t fault this place .
Thank you
Leigh and Brett

Wonderful 2 night stay on an unpowered riverside camp site. Bush camping with the security of a private park. Wonderful birds and ducks to watch, spacious grounds, good clean  ammenities (showers $1 for 6 mins) and very friendly hosts who guided us to our site. Would definitely come back!
We spent a wonderful time in Country Style Caravan Park near the dam. The owners are lovely and the place is peaceful. I recommend

We absolutely loved our stay at Country Style! It was such a pleasure chatting with the owners—they were incredibly friendly, welcoming, and couldn’t do enough for us. They went above and beyond to accommodate our travel plans and make everything work perfectly for us over the Christmas period. Highly recommend!
Camping unpowered was fantastic. The view of the river was great and the staff were so helpful and friendly. We had a great time. Thanks.

We had a quick one night stop over with our caravan on a powered site. The owners are super friendly and gave us a warm welcome, the amenities are clean. The location on the river makes for a beautiful sunset and cool down in the water. We will definitely return if passing through again!

We spent five beautiful nights camped beside the river. Lovely spot; clean amenities; well equiped and clean camp kitchen. Ben and Narellde are friendly and super helpful. Wd have been before and will go again … one of our favourite spots.

We stayed on a large, grassy drive through powered site. The park is well maintained. We had lovely views and we really enjoyed the peaceful location with birdsong as a bonus. The amenities were clean and tidy. Ben and Narelle were wonderful hosts and very attentive. We really enjoyed the Granite Belt vineyards and Girraween National Park. We highly recommend staying at Country Style Holiday Park.
Stayed here 2 nights not to bad we stayed at the unpowered sites next to the wier which is down the back great spot/walks can swim pet friendly also.

What a great place to spend a few days, if not weeks! The room was spotless and the mattress amazing (Room 2). The dog park is great, better than the council one. I'll definitely come back. Ben is simply a legend. All the facilities were clean and very organized. Thanks, Country Style Holiday Park team. See you next adventure!
Very clean and tidy, only a short drive into town for a look around.

Beautiful little spot, very clean and friendly. Parking spaces large but caution to big vans being towed by big cars, turning space tight if you gun ho it.worth the visit. Showers coin operated but one coin more than enough.

We stayed at The Country Style for 4 days in our caravan. It's a nice quiet park, the 2 sets of amenities are clean. I believe you can swim in the creek down the bottom of the park, it was too cold while we were there. It is very lovely to sit and watch the ducks. The new owners are doing a fantastic job bringing this park back to life. Well done guys. The rates are reasonable, location is great, close to any number of wineries and Stanhope. We enjoyed our stay and will be back to see the next lot of changes at the park.

The park is still fantastic!

The dam is flowing and looks brilliant, and Ben’s customer service is as exceptional as ever.

It’s always our favourite place to stay in the area.

It might not look like much from the road, but once you’re in, the park and facilities are absolutely top-notch.
Absolutely beautiful site down beside the river
Great location, lovely views, very friendly people. Amenities were in excellent condition. Monty loved the dog park
Lovely place. Plenty of space.
We camped here and will definitely camp here again. Narrell is just lovely, she makes you feel at home and is ready to help at any time.
Staff very friendly and accommodating. Amenities always clean. Great location on the Severn river, with the Glen Aplin weir.

We love our stay at this beautiful Country Style Holiday Park.The locations handy to the local market, the Christmas tree farm, the cheese and wine factory also Stanthorpe shopping centre. Our whole weekend was enjoyable and memorable. Ben, the owner very friendly and helpful. Thank you Ben and family for our comfortable stay in your park. We will definately come back in the future

An absolutely beautiful place to stay. We had an unpowered site on the banks of the water and enjoyed the views, serenity and birdlife.The owners and staff are super friendly,  the grounds are well-maintained and the amenities are immaculate. Located close to many wineries and a short drive from the shopping centre. The friendly pet goats ( who live in their own caravan) were lovely.  Low rates was the icing on the cake. Definitely one of the best caravan parks we have stayed in.

Beautiful location alongside the Severn River just south of Stanthorpe, great for a relaxing few days or just a restful overnight stay.
Wonderful camp kitchen and amenities, very friendly hosts, doggy friendly with an off-leash area...   and the enclosure with resident goats/sheep was a fun bonus.

We stayed at Country Style Caravan Park Stanthorpe in our caravan for 12 nts. On a lovely tree shaded site with views of the river. Ben was very helpful & always had a smile & kept the park clean & tidy.,we will definitely go back & stay there again

I was very impressed with the location , the facilities available to us on site were fabulous, many thanks
Dawn and Colin
Great place to relax, good sized sites.
Country feel, laid back. Clean amenities. Off leash dog park a winner for me

We had our bi annual cousins reunion at the camp ground, there was a group of 18 of us. We were in powered sites, non powered sites and cabins, all the experiences were excellent..
The facilities were clean, the grounds were well maintained and the management lovely deal with.
I highly recommend staying here when you visit Stanthorpe.

I tent camped 3 nights for a bushwalking trip to Girraween NP. Grassed sites. Quiet, spacious, great aesthetics. Roomy camp kitchen/ covered dining area. Great for groups. Amenities clean and well situated. Responsive friendly staff.
Great caravan park nice and relaxing spot super dog friendly. Had a great day.

Wow what a beautiful place on the severn river. The bird life and nature here is just superb! We camped by the river for two nights and couldn't be happier with our time at Country Style Holiday Park. Ben is great host and all round great guy. Thanks again.
Clean cabin and great facilities in a beautiful location. Mornings by the lake are the best. Would definitely stay there again.
Ben was really helpful.  Great off lead dog area.  Really nice spot to bush camp.
Had a powered campsite for a night. Quiet location with great view over the river. Ben was very easy to deal with and very accommodating. Good camp kitchen and clean amenities.
Staff are fantastic and amenities were so clean!

We booked a powered caravan site and thoroughly enjoyed our stay here. The sites were very large compared to what you get at some caravan parks, and the lake and goats were an added bonus for my son. Would highly recommend staying here

The park I found to be a great park very well laid out amenities were great, clean and very well presented. Camp kitchen well set out everything you need while you are there. Laundry had plenty of machines at a good price. Sites were great size and very easy to get in and out of. Staff were very friendly and helpful. Can’t praise the park enough if we are ever back in that area will definitely stay there again. Many thanks regards Pat and Yoko
Great spot in between everything ,walks,  pub and Stanthorpe

The location is really good. No light pollution. You can capture the milky way.  Not crowded. The facilities are really clean like the toilet. There’s a river in the site. You will  definitely love this spot. The hosts are really friendly and accommodating.

Very nice caravan park. Only stayed one night driving through. Very friendly staff and level sites. Clean bathrooms and laundry. My only comment is $1 per shower is steep as we are already paying $50 a night. Most caravanners are conscious about saving water so not sure why there is a charge. But other than that a great little park.

We spent 2 nights on a powered caravan site. Owners Ben and Narelle are very friendly, helpful and welcoming. Prices affordable. Site was lovely spot, amenities clean with good lighting at night. Definitely will return.

Beautiful park right on the river and a perfect location to explore Stanthorpe and the surrounding area. Nice clean amenities and large drive through sites in the powered section, with unpowered sites down by the river. Such a peaceful place with very welcoming hosts. Highly recommend
Excellent camping at this very well run and maintained park. Nice and quiet, great showers and helpful owners.

We had a fantastic time at Country Style Holiday Park over the weekend. The rooms were fresh, spotless, and exceeded our expectations — such a pleasant surprise! The location was perfect, offering easy access to nearby attractions while still feeling peaceful and relaxed.

The park itself is compact but cleverly designed, with plenty of space to unwind without feeling crowded. One of the highlights for our kids was feeding the goats — they absolutely loved it and it added a lovely touch to the whole experience.

Highly recommend for families looking for a clean, well-located, and welcoming place to stay. We’ll definitely be back!

8 minutes out of Stanthorpe in a lovely rural setting on the river with a weir.
Lovely cabins, warm, clean & comfortable beds.
Great dog park & camp fire pit near cabins.
Really nice stay! Thank you for everything!

How many times have you driven past this gem. What a pleasure it was to camp here. Non powered site by the river. Awesome. It's peaceful and beautiful.  Plenty of birdlife as well.
Amenties have been refurbished. Showers cost $1 for 6 minutes but worth it. Water is  hot with great pressure and  I never ran out of time.
Camp kitchen is well equipped.
There are other camping options as well. Cabins, bush camping and powered sites. Non powered by the river are best.
A great place to stop if your visiting wineries. Stanthorpe is only a short drive up the rd.
Ben and Narelle are very friendly and are doing a great job.
Ben and Narelle amazing owners of this park, it was just beautiful. Always clean and very helpful to anyone, oh do not forget the goats and sheep
Awesome place to stay , the cabins are a great size and everything you need !

We had an awesome long weekend, caravan sites are a big size and level, water pressure was great and the owners are fabulous people. They keep the park and the utilities in pristine condition and we will definitely go there again. They even persevered to win over our furbaby George the chihuahua for a cuddle. Highly recommend this park.
What a hidden gem. Ben and Narelle down to earth people. Had a great time around the communal fire pit. Views by the river stunning. Cabin was warm and clean. Bed amazing. Thanks guys.

Had a wonderful stay at Country Style Holiday Park during our Stanthorpe getaway!The park location was perfect for exploring the Granite Belt region. A clean and tidy cabin, peaceful surroundings and convenient location made our holiday comfortable and enjoyable. The owners are fantastic too. Would definitely consider staying here again when visiting the area!

The beautiful river + goats made it even better too!

Such a fantastic place to stay, we stopped in on our travels as we had a bad experience at another park in the area, and so glad we did, very clean and tidy place with good amenities, absolutely fantastic spot down near the waters edge, so relaxing and has a great atmosphere, the Park Management Ben and Narelle we so welcoming, had a fantastic night relaxing around the campfire with them and other visitors, this is definitely going to be our weekend away destination,
Highly recommended, 👌

Very welcoming wonderful bird life
Nice country feel
Amazing stars at night
Beautiful lake lovely fresh air
We had a very cozy freezing stay 😁☺️
Location is great !
Central to everything
Would highly recommend
Lovely country style caravan park! They have a lovely lake down the back & are dog friendly too! Bathrooms were greats!

We stayed in the unpowered caravan site called Willows on the river. It was spectacular. Ben and his wife were very welcoming. We had nice hot showers and clean amenities. A place we’d like to return. Thank you Geoff and Lyn

Small old style caravan park but well maintained. Large drive through sites with access to power and water. Amenities are clean and tidy but showers are $1 for 6 minutes. Laundry facilities are decent, $4 per load ($1 coins needed), dryers $6 per load. Park is right by the Severn River, unpowered camping is available right by the river but due to this access to the river is limited and views are blocked by vans on these sites. If sites were located further back from the river then everyone can enjoy the sights of the river and have access. Campfires are possible but only if you provide your own off the ground fire pit. Staff are friendly and welcoming. Only 10 minutes from Stanthorpe town center, short drive to many wineries.

Very clean amenities
6 min hot shower for a dollar coin
Fire wood available 15 kg bag for $15.00
Very well looked after
Camped there recently and enjoyed the peace and quiet clean amenities and friendly caretakers looking forward to our next visit.
A beautiful cozy environment & accomodation. We took our whole family with young child & our friends too it was perfect . Highly recommend !!  Fire pit was a highlight once kids in bed .

Ben and Narelle have created the most beautiful and welcoming countryside holiday park for anyone trying to unwind and enjoy a little nature.

The park is quiet, clean, relaxing with a tranquil atmosphere, especially having the powered site with river views. Plus they have goats! The dog unleashed area is so wonderful and having such a secure space for our chihuahuas and lab made our holiday!

Great location to easily go into town, wineries, Jam works and so much more. This is our new favourite place!
Thanks Ben & Narelle for 5 great days at your lovely park. Enjoyed it . See you again soon. Warren & Joan

Ben and Narelle are the best hosts you could want. The amenities are amazing, they are so clean. The Camp kitchen has all the appliances you have in your own kitchen at home and are very new looking. We have been here about 6 times and as we have a small camper van we like camping on the unpowered river front sites. Who needs power when you have the benefits of the Camp kitchen. We would recommend to anyone who likes the tranquility of a bush / riverside camp experience. Our relatives have stayed in the cabins here and are equally impressed with the cleanliness and service. Also our fur baby is welcome here and there is a dog park on site that lets him run around without his leash on. To sum it all up what great value you get for your money at Country Style Holiday Park.

We stayed for 1 night - 3 caravans - staff very friendly and able to accommodated us with drive through sites with short notice.
Thank You
We had an awesome stay recently. Park is very maintained, amenities are spotlessly clean and the staff were all super friendly.
Have just visited whilst picking up passenger for a wine tour, great place well appointed
We had an unpowered site with lots of room. Great views. Will definitely be back.
These guys bent over backwards to make our stay a great one very friendly great little cabin in a picturesque location we will be staying again on our next visit to stanthorpe
Very quiet ,toilets and showers very clean ,very nice outlook handy to winery and coffee shop overall.had a great week

They were able to accomodate and put our family with three vans in a row. Lovely spot, everything from wineries to town is 10 minutes.
We will be back, management was friendly and come around to have a chat with us.
Best location on Granite belt...right near the weir on Severn river...felt like we were bush camping..great atmosphere and very neat and tidy.

Cabin very cosy. Had everything we needed. Goats very entertaining. Lovely

Awesome location to say and staff are very nice

Recently stayed here for a few nights on a powered site, great size can drive through. Amenities were kept clean best iv seen in awhile. We had a great time here. The Van sites are very spacious and level and drive through. Amenities were clean. Lovely  setting with the Dam. Ben was very friendly and accommodating and we had a beautiful fire every night.

Would not recommend if you have children and are human! Beautiful holiday park with beautiful creek and tidy facilities but unfortunately the managements behaviour ruined it for us and we left early. Kids were playing while we set up our van and broke a branch off a tree, we understand the importance of the habitat around us but management yelled at the kids (without our knowledge) and the stormed in to our camp on her buggy and shoved a phone in our face with husband on the phone (who was 600km away), with no explanation other than “there’s been vandalism”. Next day we had coals for a camp oven for a birthday dinner, we put some small bits of timber on to keep the coals warm and then ducked out for a coffee (it was early). Came back to 3 buckets of water tipped on the fire which was turned to mud. Bit excessive and rude. Then proceeded to scoot around the campsite telling all the other guests about it. Hubby returned from his trip past our campsite with a courtesy skid/acceleration of his motorbike. Very unsettling for everyone, the kids were scared so we left! Not to mention we took time off our jobs, took our kids out of school and drove hours just to experience this! Very disappointing, we are absolutely reasonable people, and theres nothing that a little bit of respectful communication can’t fix!!

The country Style Holiday Park is a very comfortable site, just off the highway and a quick ten minutes into town. Enjoy the relaxed atmosphere, visit the local wineries or the hustle and bustle of beautiful Stanthorpe. Well worth the visit 🙂
What a lovely cabin. Spacious. Well equipped. Warm. A lovely outlook. Close to everything.
Stay two nights, the room is clean and bed  comfortable bathrooms are clean but don't have a hrie dry.

Beautiful, tranquil countryside . The cabin was very well equipped. Loved the walk down to the river side.  There are also a few cute goats welcome you as you walk pass them.

A lovely park on the banks of the Severn River. Our unit was clean and comfortable and had everything we needed. The park is pet friendly and has a good sized shady well fenced dog run. Very friendly and helpful hosts. We had a wonderful couple of days stay. We’ll be back

A great 5 day break,

Another enjoyable stay
Staff,  lovely people , lovely view of the lake and weir we will be back

A very clean, well appointed park.  Very helpful and approachable team made our stay enjoyable.  I would recommend a stay with Ben and his Team.  I will look forward to our next stay.

Room was good very happy with the heating. Bed comfortable and kitchen set up was good too. Only thing disappointing was water quality. Perhaps to tell visitors that the tank? Or bore water in in place. Water was very brown and we would have brought our own for drinking/ cooking had we known. Also a bit of loud traffic from hi way but understandable that can’t be helped. Great views of lake.
Ben and their team been really helpful throughout the stay, it’s been our first time camping and love the atmosphere there definitely has I’ll come back for next time, highly recommend and friendly

i really enjoyed my stay.  beautiful park near a lovely dam.  the owners take good care of this place even down to smallest details.

we will return next time.

Sensational space offered by a great husband + wife team.  Upon check in, the will give you a buggy escort to your site and leave you be to get set up & enjoy the serenity.

We had 2 set ups in the unpowered River view space  for a long weekend (3 nights) which was quiet and spacious with as promised views of the picturesque river.

Amenties available on site:
$1/ 6mins for hot showers - great pressure, clean amenties and HOT!
Coin operated full laundry facilities available. Rubbish & full bottle recycling collection on site.
Expansive undercover camp kitchen with fridges, stoves, BBQ, appliances and even a pizza oven.
Coal barrel to discard ashes/ coals from your fire pits as you pack up.
Large chess game on upper area lawn.
Dump point.
Pet friendly - Large fenced off leash dog area is super fun with plenty of space to explore, agility setup for the adventurous pups, chairs for owners to sit and enjoy and even a bag + bin station if you forget your 💩 bags.

Say hi to the animals:
The campground security guards are questionable fellas (note this is a joke, I'm referring to the goats in a pen with their own pink caravan you can say hi too).

Other Information:
Power + water sites and cabins also available at this property.  Powered sites are great size with drive through options to accommodate all rig sizes.
For those camping in the unpowered sites, you can easily and quickly fill up water tanks at council depot station in Stanthorpe (7mins from this property).  Accepts tap & pay.

Loved our set up sites but if you are opting for 5 star unpowered areas, the 7 River front sites look amazing.

Central location to all the vineyards, brewery, centre of town, servos, and beauty Stanthorpe/ region have to offer.  Handy for those travelling too, as right of the highway so easy for that nightly recharge stop.

Would definitely visit again.
Caravan site
Had a very relaxing time, the park was clean and nothing was  bother for the staff, will definitely be coming back.
Great little park, real country style and feel about the place. Staff great, amenities clean, park clean and no bloody jumping pillows etc. Well done all involved.
Lovely park, friendly helpful staff, will stay again and recommend to others
Beautiful  clean place .Lovely Manager & staff.
Great stay, lovely hosts. We stayed with our two dogs and we were very impressed with the cleanliness of the park and the ammenities.

We spent 4 days at this great location on the river with an excellent view over the water. We took our kayaks up the river to the base of the next weir wall and watched the crystal clear water coming over was an excellent sight.

Ben is a very great host
Brilliant camping spot. Close to Stanthorpe and Girraween NP

We had a wonderful experience! Traveling from Melbourne, we completely forgot about the long weekend, but when we called the site, Ben was incredibly helpful in accommodating us at the last minute. Thank you so much for your hospitality, Ben and Narelle. We’ll definitely see you both again on our next travels

We enjoyed our short stay at this lovely campsite. The location is great—peaceful and close to a river/small dam which made for a relaxing getaway. Ben went over and above to help us out to get a spot in a fully booked park! Someone cancelled last minute and he called me back immediately and got us in for the night! I was so thankful. Highly recommend

Beautiful and peaceful park on the river. Very clean and tidy.  The only thing we found  is we didn't like the drinking water so we just used our own.  Not a problem 👌. Lots of space for vans and camping. Just lovely.
Riverside location is great and peaceful
Fantastic place to relax & enjoy the outdoors! Fabulous facilities - always spotless! Really well kept property! Quiet & well run. Owners - Ben & Narelle are so lovely! Pet friendly!

We had a beautiful unpowered spot overlooking the river and weir.  Ben, the host was so welcoming and friendly. Lots of space around you, so not cramped with other campers. Quiet location, handy to Stanthorpe and surrounding towns.
Great spot and great hosts, we had a river side camping spot, and it couldn't have been better. plenty of room and closet to the water.
Lovely place, great area, Ben the Caretaker👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻 EXCELLENT value, great amenities.

CAMPERS, don't bother!  You're a second-class citizen here.

If you're an RV, then you'll be happy as you'll be hogging the view of the advertised river frontage for anyone else behind you.
Campers, don't even bother.  There absolutely no level camping sites, and you'll be lucky not to end up on gravel or any one of the many ant nests featuring some huge ants up to 2cm long with nasty pincers.

It used to be a lovely place with campers able to squeeze in anywhere that was empty, but the current delightfully customer-focussed owner, Narelle, has not considered equity or reasonable expectations of visitors in her planning the site.

The foreshore is mapped out into areas and, despite the fact there might be little spaces where one could expect to place a chair to view the sunset, you will be asked to "move on" because people are paying "Top Dollar" for those front foreshore sites.
Top dollar turns out to be $4 more than a campsite, so go figure.
A reasonable operator would allocate half of the foreshore for tent campers to create a more equitable situation.

Given that the advertising includes "pristine foreshore", this is essentially false advertising because only the lucky few up front can even access the foreshore, let alone have a view of it!

All we could see was large metal objects and 4WDs from our ant-infested campsite.

One poor camper was asked to remove his four family kayaks from the dam's edge because "it belongs to someone who's paid for that area"....so if you have watercraft you'll be expected to lug it up and down to your campsite, if you're not booked on a foreshore site.

Additionally, Narelle is possibly the rudest campsite operator we have come across.  My Italian academic visitor whom I invited to experience the delights of camping  by the river was aghast at the way I was treated by Narelle when I was simply asking her some questions about who could and couldn't access the foreshore.
Her reply to any feedback is "If you don't like it, go somewhere else", but what she fails to understand is that this is "our holiday", our experience and we have paid money on the expectation that we would be having an experience of the foreshore.

True, we can have 4 square metres on a tiny bit of land juting into the lake, and way, way over to one side of the campground, there's an area we can park ourselves for a casual sit and sip of our cocktails at sunset.

We had moved our tiny table and two chairs in between two foreshore sites to enjoy our breakfast, but this was a NO-NO.

Very un-Australian to block off foreshore to people.  We don't do this with our beaches, so why are we now denied access to one of the features which constitute the main thrust of their advertising?

Because this operator, Narelle, doesn't realise that equity is something that Australians value.

Large RV's and Caravans blocking out the entire view of the lake should not be a thing.

Place them 20 metres behind, up the slope where we campers are expected to be.  They'll still have a view over the heads of the campers and their tents.

Environmentally, their large heavy vehicles cannot be a good thing for the foreshore, especially during wet periods where I have seen it looking really ugly with great divets caused by large wheels and heavy vehicles.
Great quiet place right on the river

An amazing park only 10 mins from Stanthorpe by the river. If you are set up for off grid go to the unpowered section it's beautiful and quiet. Only a short walk to the toilets/showers and camp kitchen. The bird life is amazing. Ben is a can do happy guy who makes you very welcome. Totally recommend this place.

We loved our stay here. Great hosts and clean tidy cabin. Tavern at back fence was equally as hospitable good food, friendly relaxed staff.
We didn't stay in a cabin,  however,  the caravan sites are level and powered. Toilets & showers are absolutely spotless. Great owners,  people, great site, well worth a visit.

Great for caravans and camping 🏕
Great of leash park for your fury friend.

Stayed a couple days very pleasant good weather friendly service

Came for 6 days loved it so much booked another 8 days,the owners are so nice and friendly, the amenities are very clean and spotless, the quietness is so very soul,lifting,we bought our dogs and they have an off leash park for them, will return always when we are going to stay in Stanthorpe.

Just back from a few days at Country Style Holiday Park. Great powered site with a view of the river. Pet friendly with a fantastic off leash dog area. The amenities were super clean and very friendly hosts. Definitely will be back.

A+A+A+

See you next time.

All in all, the Country Style Caravan Park was a great place to stay for a weekend getaway to visit the Granite Belt wine region and the numerous national parks.
Extremely well kept, excellent amenities and a beautiful, natural landscape. Perfect!

The most amazing location, we stayed in a tent site by the river and it was stunning. So nice, quiet and tidy. Staff was very help and it was an easy booking process. Nice and close to Girraween Park and great value for money.

Such an amazing place to stay. We were here for 5 days and enjoyed every moment.

This park offers the full range of accommodation from off grid camping in tents or caravan/camper down on the banks of the Severn River, powered and un-powered site's as well as various styles of cabin accommodation. I can’t fault this Holiday Park, just outside Stanthorpe, amongst wineries, cheese factories, as well as pick your own strawberries and apples. This park is owned and operated by Ben & Narelle, they are very accommodating as well as the friendliest hosts we have come across in our travels. This park is also pet friendly, our boy loves it there, they also have an off leash dog park so your dogs can run free.

The greatest accolade one could offer is repeat visit to the park and we will definitely be back.

We will be back next time we are through - dogs and fires welcome which for us is an added bonus.  You can also walk across (5 min) to the winery where they have the most amazing platters for lunch.

We love our annual visit here. It's a great venue and the location is perfect for us. Very dog friendly (keep on lead) and clean amenities. Ben and Narelle are very accommodating and helpful. Already booked for next year.

We had a lovely time apart from the rain we still got out to enjoy the wineries and local produce. The hospitality was fantastic, we will definitely be back.  Hopefully get a camp spot  near the water.   Kim and Steve
We stayed 5nights over Easter and had a great time. Great park with owners that are friendly and welcoming.  Will definitely be recommending to friends.
Beautiful spot. Well-run. Good facilities.
Great campsites by the river. Helpful team at the desk and good wine across the road. I'll be back

Place is a joke, thought it was family friendly owners presented themselves as Gestapo and where very rude constantly walking past nitpicking at the kids for having fun and for us adults having a few drinks way before 10pm, will never return as the management is a absolute joke, nothing but rude towards our family! Do not recommend if your looking too have a good holiday, find somewhere else
Neat, clean and quiet what more can one say, yes I would recommend the stay as value.
My favourite camping location for this region when exploring Giraween National Park. Request a riverfront site and book early!
Beautifully furnished modern cabins, great location with onsite facilities and lagoon. Very comfortable stay, Ben was absolutely wonderful and accomodating. Will definitely be staying again
Great place to camp next to the river.  No power or water. Beautiful and peaceful.
Great Camping plac,e right on the weir. Lots of birds and facilities were great. Good Place to relax and a lot of things to see in the granite belt
We had a powered caravan site. We thought the o/nite rates were reasonable and the amenities kept clean and tidy. Great location on the Severn River near the Wier. Also not too far from town.
Great camp spot, right near the water, shade and lots of bird life. Could have a fire but too hot unfortunately. So much to see and do in Stanthorpe and surrounding area, will stay again!

Easy to pull in with caravan. Large drive through site meant we didn't need to undo the caravan for one night. Good rate for power and watered site ($41). Even though there's a tiny charge of 20c for a 3 minute shower, this is explained in the park leaflet given at check in. My only whinge would be that the grass could have been mown as the caravan sites looked a bit scraggly.
Great spot.  Very clean amenities and Ben was extremely helpful.  Nice to kayak on Severn River. Beautiful birds.  Will be back
Facilities dirty, she is not working not working, not family friendly, got told to go sit down by playing game of cricket, reception very rude overcharged and not worth your money
Ben and Narelle are the perfect hosts.  Couldn’t be more accommodating when booking late for the Australia Day weekend.  Amenities are very clean and the park well maintained.

Awesome park which has been under new ownership for past 18 months or so.
Park is well presented with large drive through powered sites or beautiful river front unpowered sites. Ammenities are immaculate as was camp kitchen.
Owners friendly and helpful.
Park close to  Stanthorpe or 40 min to Tenterfield.
If you had avoided this park when under previous owners now is the time to give them a try. You will be pleasantly surprised.

Lovely place. Very relaxing and a great base to visit sites in the Granite Belt.
Host (Ben) very organised and friendly. Amenities were the cleanest I've seen in a long while.
Swimming in the river and loads of birds around.
Will be back and will bring kayaks to explore the river.

Thanks for a great few days.
We loved our stay here

We stayed here over Christmas for 6 nights with our 2 cats and it was amazing. Was lots to see around Stanthorpe and surrounds and this was a great base. The amenities were clean and it has a laundry. Peaceful and the river was beautiful. Ben and Narelle were lovely. Will definitely be back .
A beautiful spot to stay on the riverfront. So quiet and peaceful. Enjoyed bird watching, sunsets and just being by the water. Clean amenities. Friendly owners.

Lovely, clean camp. Very professional set up by Ben. Would definitely stay again and would recommend to others.

Also dog friendly!
Highly recommend this place for quiet peace and relaxation

I don’t often give all stars but this place is Gorgeous   Ben was great with our after hours check in. Room was well appointed. For a quick overnight stay. We will include this place for a longer stay next time
Very relaxing place
Good location. Clean practical cabins. Good facilities.

Wow this Caravan Park was a pleasant surprise. We stayed in the Bush camping section right on the beautiful River. So much bird life it was amazing.
Would Definatley recommend a stay here. Will be back.

Family cabin room is tastefully decorated, clean and has more than enough bits and pieces to get you through a few days of 'camping'. The managers are friendly and helpful and the kids enjoyed biking around the sites and going to the lake for an explore. The website could do with an update, as I found some things hard to find, but the managers were happy to help with any questions I had.

We spent a wonderful week here, Narelle was so attentive, the sites are a nice size, the giant chess was a big hit with our girls and we made full use of the laundry and camp kitchen!
It was the perfect base camp to explore the Granite Belt and sit back and relax, thank you! 🙂

Not to our liking.  Well and truly overpriced for what it is.  Just being able to say you are a Big 4 when you are nowhere near the standard of most Big 4 parks should not justify the rate you charge for what amounts to little more than a dirt patch.  The 1Gb free wi-fi is so pathetic that we just gave up trying to connect.  The ridiculous notion that you don't give refunds because there "is a contract" is laughable. We have been vanning around Oz for over 4 years and have never encountered such rubbish. Even if you have a no refund policy, perhaps consider how you frame a refund refusal rather than just stating continually "you have a contract". I suggest that you use the $38 you wouldn't refund and head to Ballandean Estate to get an opportunity to see how customer service/relations works.  Offering me a customer credit to be used when I come back was totally offensive to me as I won't be back.

This would have been a good park to stay at if it wasn't for their septic system which smelt atrocious depending on the wind direction.  There were a few near misses where my wife and I came close to throwing up and looking around the campsite other guests were in the same predicament.
What a lovely lady Narelle is, warmly welcomed us late after a very long drive. Quote from the fam “they’ve really gone to trouble to make the room feel cosy”

Amenities are always kept spotless
And Narelle and the team always look after you
Super place, the river camp sites have to be seen to be believed!!

Very picturesque and peaceful unpowered sites on the banks of the Severn river.
Great location near wineries and hiking at Giraween National Park. Close to Stanthorpe. Facilities very clean.
Love this caravan park! Plenty of space, beautiful lake, very clean facilities, quiet and the owners really know how to run a park. Highly recommend and can't wait to stay again.
Quiet comfortable and clean camping. Super friendly greeting upon arrival.
Lovely little park with choice off drive through power site or off grid next to the water. Very peaceful and great price.
There is a lot of love spent on this camp ground, l love it too.
Great friendly hosts. Clean amenities.
Great place to relax. Perfect location. Quite peaceful park
Camped next to river. Awesome!

Quite and peaceful
Would recommend if looking for somewhere to unwind away from rat race.
Great place lovely view of the lake everything clean and very helpful staff member we will be going back again
The camp ground has lots space. Ben v friendly. Good spot as a base to explore the NPs the area
We had the best weekend stay, what a beautiful spot, and central to all the wineries in the region. Highly recommend a stay! Great hosts!
Very friendly helpful people. We will most certainly stay there again.

This is our second visit to Country Style Caravan Park in a powered site. Very helpful staff and amenities are very clean and well looked after. Location is great - in the middle between town and Ballendeen (wineries). Close to Girraween National Park too.
Only comment is the website alludes to Fire Pits - you need to bring your own now.  Not a big deal but needs to be updated on website to be clear; they are allowed but you need to bring your own.  Will be back again for sure!
We enjoyed our stay in the cabin. Perfectly clean, and bigger than what was expected. Fire heater is great. We were nice and warm on a freezing winter morning/night. Would book again.
I would like to thanks Bent he caravan park for his friendly service.

We stayed in a powered site and it was very peaceful. The staff at the reception are so welcoming and lovely and the bathrooms are kept very clean as well as the camp kitchen. The water tasted funny but was drinkable. Close to stanthorpe and giraween national park with lots of walks. Very lovely stay
We have been here numerous times and keep coming back..love this place and the staff are fabulous
Great caravan park just out of Stanthorpe. Friendly owners, nice wide drive thru sites. Clean facilities. Best spots are in the non powered sites near the river.
The rooms were well heated, bedding was warm and the communal fire place was a hit.

Great caravan park with super clean and ample amenities in a beautiful location by the river, as well as being close to Stanthorpe and Girraween National Park. Ben & Narelle are lovely and we will absolutely visit again if passing through the area. Some friends also stayed in a self contained cabin which was particularly awesome - clean, well kitted out, great beds, good spaces and warm ! Thanks for the hospitality !
Drive through sites
Enjoyed one night stay. Pretty spot and could stay longer. Clean amenities. Staff helpful and friendly
